title: A small thing I do to feel calm
summary: Describe one small habit that helps you feel calm during a busy day.
topic: A small thing I do to feel calm
prompt: Write 6–9 sentences about one small thing you do (not exercise, not sleep) when the day feels too busy. Use present simple. Explain why this habit helps you, using 'because'.
hintStarters: It is not a big thing, but…
sampleText: When the day feels too noisy, I make tea. I do not use a cup; I use a small glass with no handle. I hold it with both hands because the heat is part of the feeling. I sit by the window and watch the people in the street below. I try not to think about my phone. After two or three minutes, my shoulders go down on their own. It is not a big thing, but it is mine. I learned it from my grandmother, who said you cannot fix a busy heart with a busy plan.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for habits and general truths
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I make tea', 'I sit', 'I try' là hiện tại đơn dùng cho thói quen.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I make tea', 'I sit', 'I try' use the present simple for a habit.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'because' to give a reason
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I hold it with both hands because the heat is part of the feeling.' 'because' nối lý do với hành động.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I hold it with both hands because the heat is part of the feeling.' 'because' connects a reason to an action.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi một ngày trở nên quá ồn ào, tôi pha trà. Tôi không dùng tách; tôi dùng một ly thuỷ tinh nhỏ không có quai. Tôi cầm nó bằng cả hai tay vì hơi ấm là một phần của cảm giác đó. Tôi ngồi bên cửa sổ và nhìn những người dưới phố. Tôi cố gắng không nghĩ về điện thoại. Sau hai hay ba phút, vai tôi tự thả lỏng xuống. Đó không phải là điều to lớn, nhưng nó là của tôi. Tôi học được điều này từ bà tôi, người đã nói rằng bạn không thể chữa một trái tim bận rộn bằng một kế hoạch bận rộn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds most natural for describing a habit?
options: When the day is being noisy too, I am making tea.
When the day feels too noisy, I make tea.
When the day feel too noisy, I make a tea.
When too noisy day feels, tea I make.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both clauses use the present simple. 'Feels' agrees with 'the day'. 'Make tea' (no article) is the normal collocation.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for two present-simple verbs and the correct article use.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'because' correctly to give a reason?
options: I hold it with both hands, because of the heat is part of the feeling.
Because the heat, I hold it with both hands.
I hold it with both hands because the heat is part of the feeling.
I hold it with both hands because to the heat.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' + subject + verb. 'because of' is followed by a noun phrase, not a full clause.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because' you need a full clause (subject + verb).

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence with the most natural meaning of relaxing without effort.
options: My shoulders go down on their own.
My shoulders are go down by themselves.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'On their own' is the natural way to say 'without effort, by themselves'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option uses a fixed expression a native speaker would use?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence fits the topic best as a closing line?
options: Tea has many health benefits and contains antioxidants.
It is not a big thing, but it is mine.
Tea is sold in supermarkets all over the world.
Coffee is more popular than tea in many countries.
mcQuestions.explanation: Only B stays on the topic of a personal small habit; the others drift into general facts.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option still talks about *your* small habit?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think small habits like this often work better than big plans for changing how you feel?
